back_translation: In that time, a royal decree from Emperor Caesar Augustus was proclaimed, ordering a population census registration throughout the whole empire.
(This was the first population census, done when Quirinius was the provincial governor of Syria.)
Everyone traveled to register, each one to his own city.

Joseph therefore departed from the city of Nazareth in the region of Galilee, going up to the region of Judea, to the city of David called Bethlehem, because he was of the lineage and family line of David.
In order to register together with Mary his betrothed, who was pregnant.
While the two of them were there, the time came for her to give birth to the Child (royal-birth).
She bore the firstborn Son (royal-birth), wrapped the Child in swaddling cloth, then laid Him in a hay-trough, because there was no lodging place for them.
In that area there were shepherds staying out in the fields, taking shifts watching over their flocks of sheep during the night.
An angel of the Lord appeared to them, and the glory of the Lord shone brightly all around them; they were therefore afraid to the point of trembling.
The angel therefore said to them, 'Do not be afraid. Behold, we (formal-I) proclaim good news of great joy to you all, which will be joy for all the people.'
Today, a Savior has been born (royal-birth) for you all, in the city of David. He is Christ-Lord.
This will be a sign to you all: you will find an infant wrapped in swaddling cloth, lying in a hay-trough.
Suddenly, a great heavenly host appeared together with that angel, jointly praising God, saying:
Highest glory be to God, and peace on earth be to men who are pleasing to Him.
When the angels had left them, returning to heaven, the shepherds said to one another, 'Let us go to Bethlehem, to see the event that has happened, which the Lord has made known to us.'
They therefore hurried off and found Mary, Joseph, and the little infant lying in the hay-trough.
Having seen, they then narrated the words that the angel had spoken to them concerning that Child.
Everyone who heard was amazed at what the shepherds told them.
As for Mary, she kept all these events to ponder in her heart.
The shepherds returned, while giving glory and praising God, for all that they had heard and seen, just as the angel had told them.
When eight days were complete for the Child to be circumcised, His name was given as Jesus, according to the name the angel had spoken before He was conceived in the womb.
When the days required for purification according to the Law of Moses were complete, Joseph and Mary brought the Child up to the city of Jerusalem, to present Him to the Lord,
(according to what is written in the Law of the Lord: 'Every firstborn son who opens the mother's womb shall be presented as holy to the Lord'),
and to offer the sacrifice as stated in the Law of the Lord, namely: 'A pair of turtledoves, or two young pigeons.'
Behold, there was a man in Jerusalem named Simeon, a righteous and God-fearing man, awaiting the consolation of the nation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was with him.
The Holy Spirit had revealed to him that he would not die until he had seen the Christ of the Lord.
He was led by the Spirit into the temple area, and while the parents were bringing in the Child Jesus to do according to the custom of the Law for Him,
Simeon took the Child up and held Him in his embrace, then praised God saying:
O Sovereign God, now release Your servant to go in peace, according to Your word.
For my eyes have seen Your salvation,
which You have prepared before all peoples,
a light of revelation to the Gentiles, and the glory of Israel Your people.
The Child's father and mother were amazed at the words spoken concerning Him.
Simeon then blessed them, and said to Mary the Child's mother, 'Behold, this Child is appointed so that many in Israel will fall and rise, and to be a sign that will be opposed,'
'even a sword will pierce through your soul as well, so that the thoughts in the hearts of many will be revealed.'
There was a prophetess named Anna, a daughter of Phanuel, tribe of Asher; she was very aged; after leaving her father's house (marrying), she had lived with her husband for seven years.
Then she was a widow until the age of eighty-four years. She never departed from the temple, but served God night and day by fasting and prayer.
At that very moment, she approached, gave thanks and acknowledgment to God, and spoke of the Child to all who awaited the redemption of Jerusalem.
When everything required by the Law of the Lord had been completed, Joseph and Mary returned to the region of Galilee, to the city of Nazareth, their own city.
The Child grew in age and became strong in power, full of wisdom, and the grace of God was with Him.
His parents used to go to Jerusalem every year for the feast of the Passover.
When He was twelve years of age (royal), they went up according to the custom of the feast.
When the feast had ended, while they were traveling back, the Child Jesus still remained at Jerusalem, with His parents unaware.
Because they thought He was in the traveling group, they traveled a day's journey, then began searching for Him among relatives and acquaintances.
When they could not find Him, they returned to Jerusalem to search for Him.
After three days, they found Him in the temple area, seated (royal) among the teachers, listening and questioning them.
Everyone who heard was amazed at His understanding and His answers.
When His parents saw Him, they were astonished. His mother said, 'Child, why have you done this to us? Behold, your father and I have been searching for you with anxious heart.'
He (royal) replied, 'Why did you search for me? Did you not know that we (royal-I) must be in my Father's house?'
But the parents did not understand the words that He spoke to them.
He (royal) went down with His parents to the city of Nazareth, and was under their authority. As for His mother, she kept all these events in her heart.
Jesus grew in wisdom and in age (royal), was favored by God and by people.
